description_long: This is a rustic log cabin with a prominent, dark gabled hip roof and substantial overhangs, constructed primarily from large, rounded timber logs. The facade features a central entrance beneath a covered porch supported by log columns, with visible log ends at the corners emphasizing traditional craftsmanship. Windows are symmetrically arranged, with a set of three triangular windows above the entrance and additional rectangular windows along the sides, providing natural light and a balanced appearance. The cabin sits on a stone or concrete foundation, elevating it slightly above ground level. The structure includes a side porch or deck area, partially enclosed by a wooden railing, suitable for outdoor seating. The overall design is robust and inviting, blending natural wood textures with functional outdoor living spaces.
description: A robust log cabin with a dark gabled hip roof, central covered porch, and symmetrical window arrangement, featuring natural wood textures and a side deck area.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance beneath a covered porch, with visible log construction and a side deck area to the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof overhang and the central alignment of the porch, with minimal window visibility.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying three triangular windows above a log wall, with a side deck and railing visible to the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the roof's extension and the log wall, with the porch roof visible but no entrance or deck details.
attribute_roof_type: hip roof with deep overhangs, constructed from dark shingles or tiles, featuring a central ridge and symmetrical slopes
attribute_window_arrangement: Symmetrical arrangement with three triangular windows above the main entrance on the front facade, and multiple rectangular windows distributed along the sides; windows are grouped and evenly spaced for balanced aesthetics
attribute_door_placement: Single main entrance door centered on the front facade beneath the covered porch; no other exterior doors visible
